MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Jun-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kellman Hoffer for defendant and opposing party Univision Radio Inc.

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Elio Valle's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents (set #1) is granted in part. As drafted, the request is overbroad. The Court has balanced the defendant's right of privacy against the interests identified by plaintiff in the motion. In addition to the categories of documents Defendant has previously agreed to produce, Defendant is ordered to produce any and all complaints relating to discrimination, harassment, and retaliation from January 1, 2000 to the present. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting Entry And Filming Upon Deft'S Premises
Off Calendar Apr-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Heather E. Borlase and Alan B. Bayer for defendant and moving party Univision Radio San Francisco
Daniel P. Hoffer for plaintiff and opposing party Elio A. Valle
COURT REPORTER: Marianne McNair CSR #7030

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Univision Radio San Francisco's motion for a protective order is granted. As an alternative to a physical inspection, defendant is ordered to provide plaintiff with photographs of the specified areas and any plans, blueprints, schematic drawings, or any other documents that depict the areas. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.
Photos and documents to be provided in 2 weeks. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
